description_long: This object is a slender, elongated sword with a straight, narrow blade that tapers to a sharp point. The blade features a subtle wave or ripple pattern along its length, suggesting a decorative or functional design. The hilt is equipped with a prominent crossguard and a curved knuckle guard, both of which appear to be made of metal, providing hand protection. The handle is textured or ribbed for grip and ends in a rounded pommel, possibly with a small decorative accent. The coloration includes dark metallic tones with hints of bronze or copper on the guard and pommel, and a darker, possibly black or steel blade. This sword is reminiscent of a rapier or smallsword, suitable for thrusting and fencing, and would be found in urban settings as a collectible, theatrical prop, or historical artifact.
description: A slender, straight-bladed sword with a ribbed handle, ornate crossguard, and dark metallic finish, resembling a rapier or smallsword.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the full length of the blade, the crossguard, and the ribbed handle with the knuckle guard curving to the left.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the thinness of the blade and the curvature of the knuckle guard, with the handle and pommel visible in silhouette.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the crossguard, the ribbed handle, and the pommel, with the blade's tapering point visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the straight blade and the knuckle guard curving to the right, with the handle and pommel in profile.
attribute_handle_design: ribbed, cylindrical, dark material with rounded pommel and curved knuckle guard
attribute_ornamentation: subtle wave pattern on blade, metallic accents on guard and pommel
